name: QRadar-0009 - Impossible Travel and Geo-Anomaly Detection
description: Detects compromised credentials by identifying user accounts that authenticate successfully from multiple distinct source IPs within a short time window. Strong indicator of credential theft or account sharing.

detection.log_source_type: Active Directory, Azure AD, VPN Logs, Office 365, Okta
detection.event_name_filter: Successful Logon, VPN Authentication, OAuth Token Issuance
detection.aql: SELECT username, COUNT(DISTINCT sourceIP) as distinct_ips, MIN(starttime) as first_login, MAX(starttime) as last_login FROM events WHERE QIDDESCRIPTION(qid) ILIKE '%successful%' AND (QIDDESCRIPTION(qid) ILIKE '%logon%' OR QIDDESCRIPTION(qid) ILIKE '%authentication%') AND username IS NOT NULL GROUP BY username HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT sourceIP) >= 2 LAST 1 HOURS

response.offense_name: Suspicious Multi-IP Login: {username} from {distinct_ips} IPs in 1 hour
response.offense_description: User account authenticated from multiple distinct source IPs within 1 hour. Possible credential compromise or account sharing.
response.recommended_action: Force password reset, revoke active sessions, contact user to confirm activity

notes: Inspired by SIGMA rule: cloud_okta_impossible_travel. Simplified to use DISTINCT sourceIP count instead of GEO functions for broader compatibility.
author: Security Team
